name: Chow Mein (Two Sides Brown Noodles)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 pound Fresh Chinese egg noodles (regular mein)
1 pound Bok choy, cut into 1 1/2-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the mushrooms in a small bowl and pour boiling water over them. Let soak for 30 minutes.
2. Cook the noodles in a large pot of boiling water for 3 1/2 to 5 minutes. Drain, rinse under cold water, drain again, toss with a little sesame oil, and set aside.
3. Cut the scallops in half and toss with a little sesame oil and 1 1/2 teaspoons cornstarch. Refrigerate.
4. Cut the flank steak as thinly as possible across the grain. Toss with 1 1/2 teaspoons cornstarch, a little sesame oil, and the dark soy sauce. Set aside.
5. When the mushrooms have soaked, squeeze them out over the soaking liquid and reserve 1/4 cup of the liquid. Cut off the mushroom stems and discard. Cut the caps in half and set aside with the bamboo shoots and bok choy.
6. Lightly beat the eggs with a few drops of sesame oil. Heat a small frying pan, lightly oil the bottom, and pour in enough beaten egg to cover the bottom. Cook just until set, remove any excess into the uncooked eggs, cook until all the egg is used up. Stack the egg crepes, roll them up, and slice across the roll into shreds. Set aside.
7. Mix the chicken stock with the light soy sauce, 1 1/2 teaspoons salt, and sugar in a small pot. Keep warm over low heat.
8. Heat peanut oil in a wok until very hot. Lower the noodles into the oil to form a nest and cook until brown on one side (about 8 minutes). Turn the noodles over and brown on the other side (much faster). Remove to a large serving platter and keep warm.
9. Cook the scallops briefly in the same oil, remove, and drain in a colander. Turn off the heat under the oil and add the beef. Stir until the meat slices are separated and start to change color. Remove to drain with the scallops.
10. Pour off all but 1/4 cup of the oil, then heat. Add the vegetables and stir-fry for 2 to 3 minutes. Add the seasoned stock and bring to a boil. Mix the reserved mushroom soaking liquid with 2 tablespoons cornstarch dissolved in cold water and stir into the chicken stock sauce. Stir until it thickens slightly.
11. Add the beef and scallops, then the wine. Cook just until heated through. Pour over the noodles and serve garnished with the egg shreds.
